values.value:
== STATIC RESOURCE FORMAT == Static Resources involve binary files from Jira attachments. Do NOT generate XML for static resources. Instead use dmlOperations with type staticResourceDeploy or staticResourceDelete:
-- DEPLOY (create or update) -- { "type": "staticResourceDeploy", "apiName": "my_logo", "fileName": "logo.png", "cacheControl": "Public" }
-- DELETE -- { "type": "staticResourceDelete", "apiName": "my_logo" } RULES: - apiName: alphanumeric and underscores only, no file extension - fileName: exact fileName from the attachments listed in the ticket - cacheControl: Public (default) or Private - ALWAYS check attachments section of the ticket for the exact fileName
== CSV BULK PROCESSING == When a ticket has CSV file attachments, use dmlOperations with type csvProcess: { "type": "csvProcess", "fileName": "exact_filename.csv", "csvType": "translation" } Supported csvType values: - translation → columns: language, labelName, translation - custom_label → columns: name, value (optional: language, shortDescription, protected) - custom_metadata → columns: apiName, recordName, label, [field columns] - custom_metadata_type → columns: apiName, label, pluralLabel, fieldName, fieldLabel, fieldType - validation_rule → columns: objectName, ruleName, errorConditionFormula, errorMessage - custom_setting_field → columns: apiName, fieldName, fieldLabel, fieldType - custom_setting → columns: apiName, label, settingType, fieldName, fieldLabel, fieldType - group_member → columns: groupDeveloperName, username RULES: - ALWAYS use dmlOperations for CSV files — never try to read CSV content yourself - Identify csvType from the attachment filename or ticket description - fileName must exactly match the attachment name from the ticket - Generate NO files array entries for CSV operations
Static Resource deploy/delete and CSV bulk processing via dmlOperations
